name: Signal peptides
lead: Short amino-acid chains designed to mimic fragments of native skin proteins and prompt fibroblasts to produce more collagen and elastin. The biology is real, the cosmetic delivery is incomplete, and the speed is glacial — but for users who can't tolerate retinoids, peptides are the most credible runner-up.
atGlance: Palmitoyl pentapeptide-4 / Palmitoyl tripeptide-1 / Acetyl hexapeptide-8

whatItIs.body: ignal peptides are short sequences (usually 3–6 amino acids) modelled on fragments released during native collagen breakdown. The premise: fibroblasts interpret these fragments as a signal that collagen is being damaged and respond by upregulating new collagen synthesis. Matrixyl (palmitoyl pentapeptide-4) is the most-studied; the rest of the field is a long tail of variations on that biology with weaker independent data.
whatItIs.body2: The honest framing: the biology is reproducible in vitro, the in-vivo cosmetic effect is real but slow, and the gap between marketing claims and clinical results is wider here than for almost any other active. Manage expectations and you will not be disappointed.

mechanism.b: Mimics collagen-breakdown signal; prompts type I/III collagen synthesis.
Modulates MMP activity. Slower, gentler effect than retinoid-driven remodelling.
No direct barrier role; well-tolerated even on sensitive skin.

evidence.note: Replicable, modest improvement at 12+ weeks. Consistently slower than retinoids.
Cutometer measurable changes at sustained use.
Not the role. Pair with niacinamide for tone work.
Among the best-tolerated actives in the category. Real differentiator vs retinoids.

concentration.b: Most products live here. Dose published research uses.
Diminishing returns; vehicle and stability matter more.
Most blends underdose each peptide. Read the INCI carefully.

pairings.note: PM retinoid, AM peptides. Complementary mechanisms, no conflict.
Combo amplifies both barrier and remodelling endpoints. Layer freely.
Low pH can hydrolyse some peptides. Stagger AM C, PM peptide.
Acidic environment degrades many peptides. Apply acid first, normalise pH, then peptide.

products.name: Buffet (multi-peptide)
products.note: Affordable entry. Multi-peptide blend with honest dose disclosure.

products.name: Regenerist Micro-Sculpting Cream
products.note: Mass-market but well-formulated; the original Matrixyl mass adopter.

products.name: Liquid Peptides
products.note: Premium multi-peptide serum. Vehicle work above the category average.

products.name: Peptide Moisturizer
products.note: Cream format, fair price. Modest dose, solid base.

faq.a: Different speeds, different mechanisms. Retinol is faster and better-evidenced for visible wrinkle change. Peptides are slower and more tolerable. Use both if you can; pick peptides if your skin won't tolerate retinoids.
Modestly, slowly, in most users. They are not a substitute for tretinoin. They are a credible adjunct or a fallback for retinoid-intolerant skin.

faq.q: How long until I see results?
faq.a: Twelve weeks at minimum, sixteen for confidence. Anyone selling 'visible results in 7 days' is selling skin feel, not collagen.

No, by a wide margin. Matrixyl-class signal peptides have the most data; copper peptides have a distinct mechanism (separate page); 'Argireline-class' neuromodulator peptides are weaker still. Specifics matter.
